title: @manthanguptaa: I actually disagree slightly with the framing. The article says "the log is the ...
rawContent:
Tweet by @manthanguptaa (Manthan Gupta)
Author bio: ai research engineer• designing agent runtimes, memory, evals & retrieval systems for autonomous agents • dms open
Text: I actually disagree slightly with the framing. The article says "the log is the agent." I would argue the log is a durable record of what happened. It's incredibly important because it allows agent state to be reconstructed, replayed, forked, and audited.

But an agent doesn't exist in a vacuum.

Its behavior depends on files, databases, external systems, permissions, memories, and a constantly changing world that often lives outside the log.

The log is the source of truth. The agent is the state derived from it.
